title: chaenorhinum serpyllifolium
intro: Chaenorhinum serpyllifolium, also known as the fingered chamomile or fingered snailweed, is a low-growing, annual plant native to the Mediterranean region. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Chaenorhinum serpyllifolium include reducing anxiety and insomnia, as well as soothing skin irritations and wounds. Herbal preparations of Chaenorhinum serpyllifolium can be found in teas, tinctures, and infusions, and are often used in combination with other herbs to enhance their therapeutic effects.
